title: Occam’s Razor
author: From: The Great Mental Models
content: When presented with competing hypotheses, the simplest explanation is usually the correct one.
action: Simplify convoluted plans to their essential steps.

title: Inversion
author: From: Poor Charlie’s Almanack
content: Don't just think about how to succeed; think about how to fail and then avoid those things.
action: Identify failure vectors and create prevention protocols.

title: Hanlon’s Razor
author: From: The Great Mental Models
content: Never attribute to malice that which is adequately explained by stupidity or misunderstanding.
action: Substitute assumptions of malice with assumptions of misunderstanding.

title: Map vs. Territory
author: From: The Great Mental Models
content: A model is a simplification. Never mistake your mental model for the actual complexity of reality.
action: Validate models through real-world field testing.

title: Opportunity Cost (Refined)
author: From: Poor Charlie’s Almanack
content: The true cost of any action is the next best thing you could have done with that time or money.
action: Audit current tasks against the value of the next best alternative.
